steps.title: Set up the equation from the problem statement
steps.content: The problem states that group C is p% of group A. This means: $$C = \frac{p}{100} \cdot A$$ From the table, C = 690 and A = 375, so: $$690 = \frac{p}{100} \cdot 375$$

steps.title: Solve for p
steps.content: Isolate p by multiplying both sides by 100 and dividing by 375: $$p = \frac{690}{375} \cdot 100$$ To simplify the fraction, divide numerator and denominator by their GCD of 15: $$\frac{690}{375} = \frac{46}{25}$$ Therefore: $$p = \frac{46}{25} \cdot 100 = \frac{4600}{25} = 184$$
